V. LLM Review (30pts).5.1 Algorithm correctness (12): 7/12 — Implements the main rejection-sampling structure: proposes gamma draft tokens, target verifies them in one forward pass, accepts with probability min(1, p_t/p_d) when p_d>p_t, and on rejection resamples from a residual distribution proportional to max(0, p_t - p_d). However, the temperature=0 path is not the correct lossless degenerate case: it compares token_id to argmax of verify_probs (which is already one-hot from logits), and acceptance should be unconditional when p_d<=p_t (including ties) but the greedy/one-hot handling is brittle and can diverge from a true greedy target process if draft proposes a non-argmax token (it will reject, then sample from a corrected distribution that becomes effectively the argmax one-hot, but the acceptance logic is not derived from the same RS formula). Also, the implementation mixes CPU-side float extraction and per-step Python loops; while not changing math, it increases risk of subtle numerical issues. Overall: close to correct for temperature>0, but not fully rigorous/clean for temperature=0 and edge cases.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.2 Engineering quality (10): 4/10 — KV-cache handling is not zero-copy: KVCacheView clones the entire cache on creation and on clone(), and append_from_model_cache sometimes uses torch.cat reallocation. Rollback is logical-length only, but because of frequent clones/cats it is not the requested efficient slice-view rollback. Target verification is done in a single forward pass for the proposed tokens (good), but verify_probs are computed in a Python list comprehension per token and acceptance loop is fully serial with many .item() host round-trips, reducing efficiency.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.3 Completeness and robustness (8): 6/8 — Provides both baseline autoregressive sampling and speculative decoding, supports gamma, and supports temperature>0 sampling via softmax+CDF sampling. Has a temperature=0 mode, but its speculative acceptance/resampling behavior is not a clean/standard degenerate handling. No explicit EOS/stop-token handling. Random bank can exhaust for long generations. Overall runnable but missing robustness/boundary handling expected by the spec.
V. LLM Review (30pts).Overall comment: Core speculative decoding structure is present and mostly correct for temperature>0 (parallel target verification, RS accept/reject, residual resampling), but the implementation fails key engineering requirements (zero-copy KV rollback) and the temperature=0 path is not implemented in a principled, lossless way. Significant efficiency issues from cache cloning/torch.cat and Python/.item() loops.

eval_report.comment: Good. Core algorithm is basically correct, with room for improvement.
